statement: startScheduler OCC is likely duplicate-start contention from a non-atomic kickoff path, but the audit does not prove it caused lost work in the current state.
rationale: The implementation uses a non-atomic scheduled-function scan followed by runAfter(0, runScheduler), which plausibly races. Current clean stuck-work state weakens any claim that it is the present blocker.
keyAssumptions: Current clean stuck-work state is representative of the system after prior hardening.
validationCriteria: An idempotent kickoff mechanism eliminates repeated startScheduler OCC entries without harming scheduling.
Future load tests show no missed work when kickoff contention is reduced.
critiquePoints: No direct proof yet that duplicate-start OCC cannot also hide rare missed-scheduling states.
questions: What is the smallest atomic gate that removes kickoff races without broad scheduler redesign?
